steps.title: Identify the key fact
steps.content:
The passage tells us that melanosomes produce pigment and are shaped differently depending on which colors they produce. The scientists found melanosomes in a fossilized feather. The blank asks what the scientists hypothesized they could do with that shape-color relationship.
  • Choice A — 'show how melanosomes can be found in fossils belonging to animals from other extinct species.': The scientists already found melanosomes in this feather. Their goal was to determine the bird's colors, not to prove melanosomes exist in other fossils. ✗
  • Choice B — 'determine the colors of the bird based on the appearance of the melanosomes in the feather.': This applies the shape-color relationship directly: examine melanosome shape, infer color. This is exactly what the passage sets up. ✓
  • Choice C — 'explain why the melanosomes in the feather were so well preserved.': Preservation is never raised as a question in the passage. The scientists' hypothesis is about color determination, not preservation. ✗
  • Choice D — 'identify the colors of extinct animals whose fossils lack melanosomes.': Without melanosomes, the shape-color method cannot work. This is logically incompatible with the passage's method. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The shape-color relationship in the passage points directly to examining melanosome appearance to determine color. That matches choice B — B is correct.
